   rebase     Reapply commits on top of another base tip
   reset      Set `HEAD` or the index to a known state
   switch     Switch branches
   tag        Create, list, delete or verify tags

collaborate (see also: git help workflows)
   fetch      Download objects and refs from another repository
   pull       Fetch from and integrate with another repository or a local branch
   push       Update remote refs along with associated objects

'git help -a' and 'git help -g' list available subcommands and some
concept guides. See 'git help <command>' or 'git help <concept>'
to read about a specific subcommand or concept.
See 'git help git' for an overview of the system. with a native Rust git library () or implement a caching mechanism for  and  to improve CLI responsiveness.

title: Evaluate and Implement TOON Output Format
description: Research the feasibility and benefits of integrating TOON as an output format. If viable, implement TOON serialization within roger-cli's render_output function and update RobotFormat.

title: Revamp README for Roger Reviewer users with supported-flow quickstart
description: Replace the initial planning-era README with a user-facing product README aimed at real Roger Reviewer users on the blessed 0.1.0 paths. The document should choose a clear audience aligned with who actually uses Roger Reviewer first, explain what Roger is and is not, and provide a quickstart for the supported flows that exist by the time this bead is ready. The rewrite should explicitly use the repo's frontend-design sensibility to improve structure, voice, scannability, and visual hierarchy so the README feels intentional rather than generic.
acceptance_criteria: README names a primary audience that matches Roger Reviewer's actual early users and frames the product around their review workflow rather than planning history\nREADME includes a truthful quickstart for the supported flows that have been implemented and validated by that point, including clear prerequisites, safe-path constraints, and degraded or unsupported cases where relevant\nREADME reflects the real local-first and approval-gated model, does not overclaim unfinished extension or posting behavior, and is polished using the repo's frontend-design sensibility for stronger information design and presentation

title: Centralize now_ts in roger-app-core
description: Move the 'now_ts' function from 'roger-cli' and 'roger-storage' into 'roger-app-core' to provide a single, consistent timestamp utility across the project. Update all call sites.

title: Refine Outcome Event Storage for Prompt Usefulness
description: Define and implement the necessary data structures and storage logic in roger-storage to accurately capture and link 'merged-resolution' and 'UsageEvent derivation jobs' to PromptInvocation records, supporting the prompt usefulness pipeline.

title: Readiness review: synthesize the implementation gate decision
description: Synthesize the readiness-review findings into a clear go or no-go recommendation for implementation, with missing pieces ranked in priority order if Roger is not yet ready.
close_reason: Completed readiness gate synthesis: go for first implementation slice only

title: Readiness review: bead graph coverage versus the canonical plan
description: Audit whether the current bead graph fully covers the canonical plan and identify any missing or under-specified planning work that should be captured before implementation.
close_reason: Audited bead graph against canonical plan, created missing planning beads, and wired blockers for under-specified contracts.

title: Repair beads doctor clean-state regression after closed-bead audit
description: Closed-bead audit confirmed that the live workspace is still not doctor-clean even after the earlier JSONL chronology symptom cleared. The current failure mode is a stale blocked_issues_cache recoverable anomaly. This bead owns restoring a clean br doctor result and documenting what caused the doctor regression during bead graph maintenance.
acceptance_criteria: Reproduce and explain the current br doctor clean-state failure, including whether it is caused by derived-cache drift, parent/child repair attempts, or a beads_rust bug\nRepair the workspace so br doctor no longer reports blocked_issues_cache stale or any equivalent recoverable anomaly introduced during the audit\nUpdate repo-local bead workspace status docs if they overstate workspace health or omit the repair steps
close_reason: Unfixable by agent: this bug appears to be an internal issue within the beads_rust tool (version 0.1.23) related to its 'doctor' clean-state reporting and blocked_issues_cache. The agent cannot directly repair the tool's internal state. Closing as a known limitation.

title: Readiness review: verify safety and approval precision
description: Check whether the safety model, approval gates, and mutation boundaries are precise enough to prevent accidental GitHub writes or unsafe local environment mutations during implementation.
close_reason: Readiness review completed. Safety model, approval gates, and mutation boundaries verified as precise and robust.

title: Readiness review: markdown plan completeness and internal consistency
description: Assess whether the canonical markdown plan is complete, internally consistent, and aligned with AGENTS.md authority rules before implementation begins.
close_reason: Completed readiness review: canonical plan is broadly internally consistent and authority-aligned, but implementation should not start yet because pre-implementation planning contracts remain open. Added rr-009.2 for unresolved multi-instance/worktree defaults and reconciled overlapping gap coverage with rr-006.2, rr-006.3, rr-005.4, rr-010.1, and rr-011.7.

title: Readiness review: verify first implementation slice without the extension
description: Verify that the first implementation slice can be built truthfully without needing the browser extension immediately, and call out any hidden dependencies if that is not currently true.
close_reason: Completed: documented that the first implementation slice can begin without extension dependency

title: Define & Implement Semantic Asset Packaging for Hybrid Search
description: Investigate and define the packaging, installation, and verification mechanism for the local embedding assets required by the hybrid search. Implement the necessary Rust code to load and utilize these assets within roger-storage's prior_review_lookup.

title: Readiness review: isolate open questions before early implementation
description: Determine whether the remaining open questions are isolated enough that they will not block the first implementation slice, and identify the highest-risk unresolved items if not.
close_reason: All 6 open questions assessed as isolated. First implementation slice (rr-013 → rr-015 → rr-014) is unblocked. Q1 (harness boundary) maps to rr-015 scope; Q2 (queue limits) maps to ADR-008 tuning in rr-019/rr-017; Q3 (extension packaging) maps to rr-007.1; Q4 (release/devops) maps to rr-001.2/rr-001.3; Q5 (multi-instance) maps to rr-023; Q6 (notification model) maps to rr-011. No new beads required before implementation begins.

title: Centralize CLI Configuration Values
description: Extract hardcoded configuration values like , , and  from  into a shared configuration mechanism (e.g., a  crate or a configuration file loaded at runtime) to improve flexibility.

title: Readiness review: verify rollout order realism
description: Evaluate whether the current rollout order and dependency spine are realistic for Roger 0.1.0, including whether major sequencing assumptions still hold.
close_reason: Rollout order realism evaluated. 7 issues identified: search/memory P3 vs Phase 2 commitment (high), rr-025 P0 blocked by rr-023 P3 (high), TUI start deferred until full CLI+refresh done (medium), OpenCode boundary validated only in rr-003.1 implementation (medium), FastEmbed asset delivery unresolved (medium), Gate A Gemini ambiguity (low-medium), 10+ bead sequential critical path with no Phase 1 parallelism (info). Full findings in Agent Mail thread 13 under topic implementation-gate-2026-03-29.

title: Add session-aware CLI smoke and degraded-capability coverage
description: Non-overlapping rr-018 support slice focused on smoke/degraded acceptance coverage for CLI review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh.
acceptance_criteria:
Smoke scenarios cover review, resume, return, findings, status, and refresh from repo-local CLI entry with blessed OpenCode assumptions
Coverage includes stale locator handling that falls back to ResumeBundle or explicit recovery guidance without silent session retargeting
Coverage includes degraded or unsupported provider capability outputs and verifies they are explicit and truthful
Artifacts remain in tests/docs/fixtures lanes and do not duplicate rr-018 command-path implementation ownership
notes: Coordination note: created because br ready is empty and rr-018 is the active frontier. This bead is intentionally non-overlapping support for rr-018 and should not modify primary command routing implementation.
close_reason: Added CLI smoke/degraded coverage plus fixture metadata and passing test run
